name: CD4 cytotoxic T cells targeted CMV-antigen-positive senescent human skin fibroblasts
summary: Human skin evidence supports a natural immune-clearance axis, but it remains observational and ex vivo.
statement: In human skin, increased CXCL9 and cytotoxic CD4 T-cell recruitment were associated with reduced senescent fibroblast accumulation, and skin-resident CD4 cytotoxic T cells eliminated HCMV-gB-positive senescent fibroblasts through an HLA-II-dependent mechanism ex vivo.
population_or_model: Human skin samples and ex vivo skin-resident CD4 cytotoxic T-cell assays
time_horizon: Cross-sectional human skin and ex vivo immune-recognition assays
quantitative_note: The source reports that senescent fibroblasts increased in old versus young skin but did not continue increasing with advancing age among elderly samples, consistent with an immune-control mechanism.
caveats: This is not a tested therapeutic intervention.
The CMV-antigen mechanism may depend on infection history and tissue context.
A patent application is disclosed for cytomegalovirus-vaccine use in aging-associated diseases.
